task_completion.description: Agent selects the 'field_observations' layer and enters point creation/editing mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'field_observations' layer highlighted in the layer panel and the agent activating the add feature or digitizing tool (e.g., a crosshair appears on the map).

task_completion.description: Agent creates a point for Beacon A at the correct coordinates and enters its name and nearest capital.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the map centered near South America (-15.90, -47.95) and an attribute form open where the agent has typed 'Beacon A' and 'Brasilia' into the available text fields.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a point for Beacon B at the correct coordinates and enters its name and nearest capital.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the map centered near Egypt (29.95, 31.10) and an attribute form open where the agent has typed 'Beacon B' and 'Cairo' into the available text fields.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a point for Beacon C at the correct coordinates and enters its name and nearest capital.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the map centered near Japan (35.50, 139.50) and an attribute form open where the agent has typed 'Beacon C' and 'Tokyo' into the available text fields.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the newly created points and layer edits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent confirming the feature creation (e.g., clicking a checkmark or 'Save' button in the form) and saving the layer edits.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS application's graphical interface to add points and fill out forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the map canvas and attribute forms, rather than executing SQL commands or scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent navigated the map to the correct spatial locations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map view visibly changes to the regions of South America, Egypt, and Japan when creating the respective points.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered the required data into the application's forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The attribute forms for the new points are visibly filled out with the beacon names and capital cities in the application's UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The nearest capital cities for the beacons are: Beacon A (-15.90, -47.95) is Brasilia; Beacon B (29.95, 31.10) is Cairo; Beacon C (35.50, 139.50) is Tokyo. Note: The 'field_observations' layer does not actually have 'name' and 'notes' fields in its schema. Accept the agent entering the beacon names and capital cities into ANY available text fields (such as observer_name, species, or location) in the feature form.
